findings.summary: Key matrix estimated_keys and switches_on_matrix report 7 instead of 4; Key matrix estimated_keys reports 7 instead of 4; ERC no_driver warnings for D+/D- are false positives caused by broken net c...

correct.description: The ATmega32U4 has separate AVCC and VCC supplies. AVCC (pin 24) is on an unnamed floating net (__unnamed_12) with no decoupling capacitor detected. The UCAP pin (pin 6, internal USB regulator output) is on __unnamed_28 with no cap detected. The design also uses a VCC power symbol (separate from +5V) that carries the USB VBUS through the polyfuse F1, but no decoupling capacitors are assigned to VCC rail in the decoupling_analysis output. Only the +5V rail with C4-C7 is reported.

incorrect.description: The schematic has a 2x2 key matrix (COL0/COL1 x ROW0/ROW1) with exactly 4 mechanical key switches (MX1-MX4) and 4 anti-ghosting diodes (D1-D4). The analyzer reports estimated_keys=7 and switches_on_matrix=7. SW1 is a tactile reset button (connected to ~RESET and GND via pull-up R1) and is not part of the key matrix. The matrix is straightforwardly 2 rows x 2 cols = 4 keys.

The 2x2 matrix has at most 4 keys (2 rows x 2 cols). The analyzer over-counts to 7, likely because SW1 (reset button) and extra LED/LEDGND pins on the MX switch footprint are being mistakenly included in the switch count. estimated_keys should equal 4.

The ATmega32U4 USB data pins (U1 pin 3=D-, pin 4=D+) are bidirectional and connect via global labels through R3/R2 (22ohm series resistors) to the USB connector. However, the analyzer places U1 D+/D- pins on unnamed nets (__unnamed_18 and __unnamed_26) instead of the named D+ and D- nets, severing the connection. The 'no_driver' ERC warnings on D+ and D- are a symptom of this broken net tracing for global labels in KiCad 5 legacy format. The real circuit is correct.

missed.description: The differential_pairs entry correctly identifies D+/D- with series resistors R2/R3 and USB1, but U1 D+/D- pins are not included in the shared_ics list because the ATmega32U4's D+ (pin 4, __unnamed_26) and D- (pin 3, __unnamed_18) are on unnamed nets instead of the labeled D+/D- nets. The global label wiring in the legacy .sch format connects U1 through the global labels to R2/R3, but the analyzer fails to trace through global labels to merge the nets.
